title: Difference between INTRUMENTALITY and PRE INTRUMENTALITY
display_title: Difference between INTRUMENTALITY and PRE INTRUMENTALITY
wikitext:
[]: Difference between 'INTRUMENTALITY' and 'PRE-INTRUMENTALITY'             Quote Post by Kharis1212 » Tue Dec 09, 2025 7:44 am This is a actually is a reply to ( post/945605/Question-about-a-scene-in-EoE/#945605 ) Because I wanted to oppose the notion "Maybe his honesty and change was the reason Asuka returned to him at the beach." As this would mean that Asuka already knew that and she came to Shinji and accepted him not because she accepts herself but rather because Shinji accepts himself.because the action should be based on her own character arc.since Evangelion is about accepting one's self ,not about Shinji accepting pain and ambiguity in totality. Because We know Asuka hates and rejects her weak part and do hates and rejects Shinji to as he is her shadow self. So when Asuka touches Shinji's cheeks in the last and shows affection(acceptance of Shinji), Symbolically She accepted herself to.As her rejection of herself is the main point of her hedgehog's dilemma.And this shows that She had resolved her hedgehog's dilemma onscreen(Shinji had done that onscreen resolve by rejecting intrumentality). As i don't know if I am correct or not.But I think people from story board did accepted that Asuka was watching if Shinji is real or not just like Shinji was doing. Which again validated my saying. And she coming to him and then accepting him because he accepted himself is little out of theme for that(she should come to him and accept him because she accepts herself now). Now during this whole analysis.I became doubtful about if Intrumentality is any different from PRE-INTRUMENTALITY or more simply. *Is it harder to retain one's self in intrumentality than in PRE-INTRUMENTALITY* ? Because in my head canon,Asuka shouldn't properly know what Shinji did during intrumentality as she doesn't even have special privilege like Shinji.And there should be more(intensity of) melding of mind during intrumentality than PRE-INTRUMENTALITY and now unlike Pre-Intrumentality where you were connected to few individuals.Now you are connected to the whole human population.Meaning *It should be hard to retain one's self( consiusness) in intrumentality than PRE-INTRUMENTALITY by several or even thousands of more folds*. By this Asuka shouldn't know what Shinji did and if she detected that,she shouldn't remember much due to too much melding of mind and several millions more individuals melding together. And whatever she remembers about Shinji in the end should come from PRE-INTRUMENTALITY where the mental load is far far less than real Intrumentality. However she might remember Shinji started the intrumentality project since at that time it was PRE-INTRUMENTALITY. So apart from everything the main question is now. What is the difference between Intrumentality and pre+intrumentality?How much consciousness one might retain during each intrumentality?How much aware of what happened during intrumentality and PRE-INTRUMENTALITY one might be after experiencing both respectively(difference of awareness)(or simply difference of awareness during each Intrumentality)? You guys can simply go for title to if you are too confused as to what I asked. I have zero research about this and neither do I have any time.i ask you guys.

[]: Re: Difference between 'INTRUMENTALITY' and 'PRE-INTRUMENTALITY'             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Wed Dec 10, 2025 11:04 am Kharis1212 wrote: As this would mean that Asuka already knew that and she came to Shinji and accepted him not because she accepts herself but rather because Shinji accepts himself.

[]: Re: Difference between 'INTRUMENTALITY' and 'PRE-INTRUMENTALITY'             Quote Post by Kharis1212 » Thu Dec 11, 2025 5:09 am ...... What made you think that he was looking to merge with Asuka?on an honest level.Though actually if I say that “Shinji rather than merging with her was asking for complete acceptance, emotional support and being completely transparent about intentions” and even saying “Shinji wanted to merge with Asuka”.Both are very correct reading and unarguable.Because Evangelion wants ambiguity so people can make there own interpretations. Because in my head Shinji is not wanting a merger completely because merging with someone is death and he is trying to evade death by Asking help from Asuka. Brain treats mental state and a connection as survival.Since Shinji is thinking he will die("don't kill me") he got to Asuka to ask help(during hospital)And it was crystel clear that before the dirty action.Shinji was asking for acceptance and basic emotional support from Asuka rather than "Hey Asuka merge with me cuz I am feeling shit”,Now you can give argument about that he did what he did which points towards merger,But when you put psychology on line,it becomes illogical to say that masturbating over comatoes means wanting to merge because action itself becomes a replay of residuary memory(and if wanna debate further on this,go and debate in the “Masturbating over comatoes” thread), because symbolisms cannot always mean character intentions,but the psychology always means character intentions.and it is used during that scene. So since Shinji is trying to evade death i.e complete merger,he should not be going for merge itself until he actually wants to suicide,which he does when he accepted intrumentality(his suicide).And hence during pre-intrumentality he is asking for emotional support and acceptance(yeah I repeated this like hundred of times,but I am last .. to edit this as it's hard to write such complex things). So noting his interaction in hospital scene,It becomes Far less likely that he is actually Asking her to merge with him in during pre-intrumentality specifically.Because critiques usually ask you to not interpret every sexual imagery as charcater intentions even it if means merging(For example we see naked trinity smiling and being naked,where boobs symbolise comfort and smile ambiguity) as they are usually for pure purpose of symbolism that Shinji wants comfort etc. So it's more likely that Shinji is asking her for unconditional emotional support and acceptance(without doing anything for her). And for intrumentality.Now the wish to merge actually becomes partially true. But we also need to remember,That here Shinji wants to die rather than being merged with Asuka or any of trinity Because we know when intrumentality will complete,Every single individual will die to merge into a single being, There's no way a single being will born if they don't merge. Again showing his affinity is more towards dying than,merging. As even komm Susser Tod further strengthens this notion,though it's still interpretational,so can't argue. Again Asuka's rejection is not only composed of Shinji wanting to use her as emotional crutch.But also because Shinji reminds her of her own broken self.Thats why she was lashing out so hard,even physically.Because we also need to remember that whatever Asuka said about Shinji,also perfectly applies on herself unarguably. Well first.Isnt it a little doubtfull.That Asuka rejects Shinji when she doesn't accepts herself and rejects her weak part,But at the end she does accepted Shinji when she accepts herself.*Because if she didn't accepted herself,No matter how much one argues,she wouldn't have accepted Shinji and would have done similar to what she did in previous interaction(script) of i need you. But she didn't.It means her acceptance of Shinji is based on her acceptance of her own weak part. Hence disproving the notion that she accepted him because he accepted himself. However your notion still goes for Asuka returning to Shinji unarguably. Now why i don't like your reading is because it just shows like she had already solved her hedgehog's dilemma.rather than showing her a traumatic person like Shinji who is dysfunctional and is actually trying to learn. When main point of her own dilemmas is self acceptance,and acceptance of pain and ambiguity like Shinji. Though controle over suicidal thoughts is another story.Because unlike Asuka who wanted her mother,Shinji wanted *intimacy* mostly,and is probably very confused about his mother.As intimacy is best thing you can have against loneliness. Now.Why Asuka's rejection was also composed her own hatred towards herself with similar or equal weightage as the reason that Shinji wanted to use her as a crutch. Now your previous saying were already pretty much weakened that he wanted to merge with her.Cuz merge is something he actually should avoid.that is death.And at that moment he is saving his life by asking for help rather than merge itself. Now if Asuka wanted to reject Shinji,There are lots of ways.For example giving example of her own broken self which will make Shinji rethink why he actually wants help? Now the simple answer is her rejection is just too harsh.If you look very closely to the each dialogue.You will find out that Shinji is not the only person who is unbalanced mentally(pardon my English) or simply he is not the only one who is trying to protect himself. (“The violence/bitterness of her lines and gestures (the pushing-away, the tone, the focus on his flaws rather than comfort) fits classic projection: people attack in others what they cannot accept in themselves. Her refusal to meet his need — despite knowing his despair — reads as self-protective (I will not be the outlet that confirms my own weakness). Film scholars note the cruelty is part of her defense mechanism. ”) (Asuka’s lines in the “hell kitchen” / pre-Instrumentality exchange hit directly at Shinji’s perceived laziness, cowardice, and neediness: “You don’t truly understand” / “You think you can save me?” / “Don’t come near me.” Those are accusatory lines that map onto Asuka’s own deepest self-doubts (fear of dependence, need for validation). The script/scene transcripts show this blunt, repeated confrontation. ”) Again argument is not that she should accept him absolutely.Its that Asuka's rejection is too harsh than it should,And being angry isn't a good reason. It's clearly that she is uncomfortable because Shinji is her shadow self.Its not like anger is not there.But the source of anger and the reason for such lashing out seems to be deffensive act. Rather than harsh rejection ,like she she first said things like “...... You are afraid of your mother.......”(whole dialogue) is a attack on his ego and his self image.And “Pathetic” and “No” is actually complete rejection of him but mostly it showed him how un needed and how he don't deserves to exist(the reason why called it attack is because there is difference between explaining and attacking).These 2 combined made him commit symbolic suicide(it's not that he is not in blame).No matter how pathetic one can be.They don't deserve a complete rejection in worse state of mind this way.What Shinji at that moment needed was *truth with compassion**That even if he is flawed he should exist*.As Asuka showed all his flaws like he shouldn't exist.Like he is un needed.And even is it teaches something,it was harmful and indeed maliciously meant to be harmful.Thoigj i don't think she was teaching either. Though i know you start arguing that it wasn't malicious or simply trying to reject the whole thesis..(Malicious because due to intrumentality she perfectly knows Shinji's state and she is actually,more rational than you think,but her ability to empathise was mitigated,but rationality still means she should have some idea what consequences can be.). Now importance of shoving 3 large paragraphs just on her insecurity is to show that her rejection partially contains insecurity of Shinji being her shadow self. Like think about it.How she was reacting during episode 22' to know her weak self.And now she is watching literal mirror. Hence the two(3 actually,One against the notion Shinji wants to merge,One about the chronology of Asuka's acceptance and rejection of Shinji and at last Asuka's insecurity for Shinji being a shadow selve) arguments show that Asuka's acceptance of Shinji mostly comes from Her acceptance of her self(as Shinji is still Shinji in the end). However I think I have to partially accept the notion that Asuka came to Shinji in the end because Shinji accepts herself.But as I said just partially as her acceptance of herself Still should have some impact. And also I guess I accept your say about the intrumentality to. Now purpose wasn't to just shove blame on Asuka.Its just Shinji is already very welljustified character and I don't feel a need to say anything,like I can say that during hell kitchen scene he could have took Asuka's word( actually i don't have time and don't want to make this even more longer than this is).

[]: Re: Difference between 'INTRUMENTALITY' and 'PRE-INTRUMENTALITY'             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Thu Dec 11, 2025 9:28 pm Kharis1212#945652 wrote: Because in my head Shinji is not wanting a merger completely because merging with someone is death and he is trying to evade death by Asking help from Asuka.

[]: Re: Difference between 'INTRUMENTALITY' and 'PRE-INTRUMENTALITY'             Quote Post by Kharis1212 » Fri Dec 12, 2025 4:14 am Weird_ocean..... Your are saying same thing again........ It's necessary Shinji wants a merger with Asuka and die literally during pre-intrumentality? pre-intrumentality?Man i can agree with you in case of intrumentality.. .....but why pre-intrumentality when he is not suicidal yet didn't suicide?As I said earlier that Shinji should be trying to not die “dont kill me” and merging with someone is die. Now it's not necessary that merging with someone is the only merging in this visual language. *Intimacy* fits better in this case.Because with visual language one also has to maintain logic behind character intentions.Or the scene might become straight shit because it can't even support a basic logic.its not necessary sexual language during pre-intrumentality means he wants to have sex.Like think logically is sex enough?Sexual visual also symbolises *intimacy* and in my opinion it makes far more logic to use intimacy logic in PRE-INTRUMENTALITY. Now let me debunk your Logic with Shinji's behaviour even before Pre-Intrumentality.First of all Shinji has a very bad mental state before pre-intrumentality so you simply can't blame him for anything on simpler level.He is in survival state which means you simply can't expect him to think about for others and it's laughable if u wanna blame him for that.Same goes for Asuka. (Severe depression, severe and Constant anxiety,total loss of coping mechanism,emotional collapse or maybe emotional fragmentation,exhaustion,suicidal state, Moderately and sometimes in severely Dissociative state without any cause(yeah just look in his eyes,he only reacts to high pitch sounds or if you shake him too much.).) First of try understand Shinji's pattern through the movie.Starting from hospital scene.We can see that Shinji is asking help from Asuka(Note that Shinji is in a aroused state,in front of Asuka,and aroused mean hyper activeness or maybe he is having anxiety).If he is asking help it means he indeed has will to live but in crumbled state. Or simply whenever Shinji is with Asuka he gets a hope of living.Now after hospital scene when Shinji thought that nobody will help him,then he actually got suicidal,as he lost hope,As the only hope i.e Asuka wasn't reacting.Hence it creates a illusion that he 'just wanna die' when ‘his suicidal thoughts varies with hope of getting help’.Now after when we get to Pre-Intrumentality.We see that now a part of hope again come sto Shinji.He is again hyper activeness around Asuka and is constantly asking for help.Meaning now he is again having hope of living.And he is actually waiting for a proper answer.Because now he will get a chance to live if Asuka accepts the offer. But when Asuka finally rejects him.With rejection she also made him feel like shit(worse thing you can do to a suicidal guy).He finally snaps and Suicides.As now he is sure that he will die. A very simple pattern.On basis of this it again becomes less likely wants to merge with Asuka at that moment.As he is not perfectly suicidal at that point of time but rather still has hope that he can be alive if she accepts him.And if he has hope,why would he wants to die by merging with her?When she accepts to live with him?To have intimacy with him? Even lyrics like orignal and Everything you've ever dreamed showed that Shinji wants to have intimacy rather than a merger.and it's secondary canon. “I wanted to kiss her ...” poetically either means to have intimacy or to express love. Now in intrumentality,As i said earlier that I can agree with you.But you also have to agree that both reason exists.That he wants to die and also merge. Now why I said that he wanted to die earlier is because when he starts intrumentality.There is not a single doubt and is unarguable.That he wants death of his own.And destruction to.“I shouldn't exist to”. That's why I said the reason of start of instrumentality was his wish to die.However his wish to merge can be true later when intrumentality.A state where there is no loneliness and barrier.As he might even know that he will die when the whole process completes(idk if the process was completed or not, because becoming one means death for sure). And again ............ What made you think.... That he wanted to merge into pre-intrumentality.Because visual language doesn't makes sense with what character intentions actually should be. And visual you are talking about don't necessarily mean he wants to merge. I am not saying that his wish to merge doesn't exist.its just intimacy angle fits better and logical.Since again at last. Dialogues suggests too that he wants to live(when Asuka is around)(hope) .But again that hope is what you call the final thing that can stop him from deing. For example “Be nice to me”.Suggests that.However...... next dialogues are ambiguous and it supports both interpretations.By and by you.

[]: Re: Difference between 'INTRUMENTALITY' and 'PRE-INTRUMENTALITY'             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Fri Dec 12, 2025 6:09 am Kharis1212 wrote: Now let me debunk your Logic with Shinji's behaviour even before Pre-Intrumentality.First of all Shinji has a very bad mental state before pre-intrumentality so you simply can't blame him for anything on simpler level.He is in survival state which means you simply can't expect him to think about for others and it's laughable if u wanna blame him for that.Same goes for Asuka.

[]: Re: Difference between 'INTRUMENTALITY' and 'PRE-INTRUMENTALITY'             Quote Post by Kharis1212 » Fri Dec 12, 2025 8:21 am Welll it's hard to put things in quotes you know.I still don't have much idea how to do it.sorry. Well I guess I agree with you now. But the thing is.Wouldnt both lyrics of Everything you've ever dreamed be 2ndary level of canon as it is author approved? I mean even i actually agree that it's not primary.But it should be 2ndary level. Why because even if the lyrics can't be used to explain the situation,alr 'situation' of eoe.But Shouldn't be lyrics still usefull? Because the main purpose of the lyrics are to understand the relation between Asuka Shinji.As I mentioned “I wanted to kiss her,but I was hurt” explains like entire thing Shinji feels for Asuka without contradicting anything(interpretational) though the next lines become a little deviated in criteria of narrative,but still remains intact when it comments on there relation specifically .Meaning rather than what it comments on the 'situation', shouldn't it's comment be correct when we use this to understand the relation between Asuka and Shinji? And you don't even get any problem related to narrative when you talk About the official version of Everything you've ever dreamed (yeah it's my favourite).As it only comments on events till the strangling of Asuka in the hell kitchen scene. Because I use these lyrics a lot.Because it's very handy to explain what Shinji feels for Asuka.And is better than actually looking at the main series where he fails to help her.Like come on failure to help has something to do with how he feels for her.But not everything.He is a very traumatized guy,you can't judge him like that when he was actually spiralling down himself. Aa these lyrics give enough level of canon that I can contradict any yap out of air for sure.

[]: Re: Difference between 'INTRUMENTALITY' and 'PRE-INTRUMENTALITY'             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Fri Dec 12, 2025 8:38 pm Kharis1212#945667 wrote: But the thing is.Wouldnt both lyrics of Everything you've ever dreamed be 2ndary level of canon as it is author approved?

As this would mean that Asuka already knew that and she came to Shinji and accepted him not because she accepts herself but rather because Shinji accepts himself.because the action should be based on her own character arc.since Evangelion is about accepting one's self ,not about Shinji accepting pain and ambiguity in totality. Because We know Asuka hates and rejects her weak part and do hates and rejects Shinji to as he is her shadow self. So when Asuka touches Shinji's cheeks in the last and shows affection(acceptance of Shinji), Symbolically She accepted herself to.As her rejection of herself is the main point of her hedgehog's dilemma.And this shows that She had resolved her hedgehog's dilemma onscreen(Shinji had done that onscreen resolve by rejecting intrumentality). As i don't know if I am correct or not.But I think people from story board did accepted that Asuka was watching if Shinji is real or not just like Shinji was doing. Which again validated my saying. And she coming to him and then accepting him because he accepted himself is little out of theme for that(she should come to him and accept him because she accepts herself now). Now during this whole analysis.I became doubtful about if Intrumentality is any different from PRE-INTRUMENTALITY or more simply. *Is it harder to retain one's self in intrumentality than in PRE-INTRUMENTALITY* ? Because in my head canon,Asuka shouldn't properly know what Shinji did during intrumentality as she doesn't even have special privilege like Shinji.And there should be more(intensity of) melding of mind during intrumentality than PRE-INTRUMENTALITY and now unlike Pre-Intrumentality where you were connected to few individuals.Now you are connected to the whole human population.Meaning *It should be hard to retain one's self( consiusness) in intrumentality than PRE-INTRUMENTALITY by several or even thousands of more folds*. .Now you are connected to the whole human population.Meaning *It should be hard to retain one's self( consiusness) in intrumentality than PRE-INTRUMENTALITY by several or even thousands of more folds*. By this Asuka shouldn't know what Shinji did and if she detected that,she shouldn't remember much due to too much melding of mind and several millions more individuals melding together. And whatever she remembers about Shinji in the end should come from PRE-INTRUMENTALITY where the mental load is far far less than real Intrumentality. However she might remember Shinji started the intrumentality project since at that time it was PRE-INTRUMENTALITY. So apart from everything the main question is now. What is the difference between Intrumentality and pre+intrumentality?How much consciousness one might retain during each intrumentality?How much aware of what happened during intrumentality and PRE-INTRUMENTALITY one might be after experiencing both respectively(difference of awareness)(or simply difference of awareness during each Intrumentality)? Because in my head Shinji is not wanting a merger completely because merging with someone is death and he is trying to evade death by Asking help from Asuka. Brain treats mental state

Article: Difference between INTRUMENTALITY and PRE INTRUMENTALITY
.Because Evangelion wants ambiguity so people can make there own interpretations. Brain treats mental state and a connection as survival.Since Shinji is thinking he will die("don't kill me") he got to Asuka to ask help(during hospital)And it was crystel clear that before the dirty action.Shinji was asking for acceptance and basic emotional support from Asuka rather than "Hey Asuka merge with me cuz I am feeling shit”,Now you can give argument about that he did what he did which points towards merger,But when you put psychology on line,it becomes illogical to say that masturbating over comatoes means wanting to merge because action itself becomes a replay of residuary memory(and if wanna debate further on this,go and debate in the “Masturbating over comatoes” thread), because symbolisms cannot always mean character intentions,but the psychology always means character intentions.and it is used during that scene. So since Shinji is trying to evade death i.e complete merger,he should not be going for merge itself until he actually wants to suicide,which he does when he accepted intrumentality(his suicide).And hence during pre-intrumentality he is asking for emotional support and acceptance(yeah I repeated this like hundred of times,but I am last .. to edit this as it's hard to write such complex things). So noting his interaction in hospital scene,It becomes Far less likely that he is actually Asking her to merge with him in during pre-intrumentality specifically.Because critiques usually ask you to not interpret every sexual imagery as charcater intentions even it if means merging(For example we see naked trinity smiling and being naked,where boobs symbolise comfort and smile ambiguity) as they are usually for pure purpose of symbolism that Shinji wants comfort etc. So it's more likely that Shinji is asking her for unconditional emotional support and acceptance(without doing anything for her). And for intrumentality.Now the wish to merge actually becomes partially true. But we also need to remember,That here Shinji wants to die rather than being merged with Asuka or any of trinity Because we know when intrumentality will complete,Every single individual will die to merge into a single being, There's no way a single being will born if they don't merge. Again showing his affinity is more towards dying than,merging. As even komm Sus

Again Asuka's rejection is not only composed of Shinji wanting to use her as emotional crutch.But also because Shinji reminds her of her own broken self.Thats why she was lashing out so hard,even physically.Because we also need to remember that whatever Asuka said about Shinji,also perfectly applies on herself unarguably. Well first.Isnt it a little doubtfull.That Asuka rejects Shinji when she doesn't accepts herself and rejects her weak part,But at the end she does accepted Shinji when she accepts herself.*Because if she didn't accepted herself,No matter how much one argues,she wouldn't have accepted Shinji and would have done similar to what she did in previous interaction(script) of i need you. When main point of her own dilemmas is self acceptance,and acceptance of pain and ambiguity like Shinji.  rather than merge itself. Now if Asuka wanted to reject Shinji,There are lots of ways.For example giving example of her own broken self which will make Shinji rethink why he actually wants help? Now the simple answer is her rejection is just too harsh.If you look very closely to the each dialogue.You will find out that Shinji is not the only person who is unbalanced mentally(pardon my English) or simply he is not the only one who is trying to protect himself. (“The violence/bitterness of her lines and gestures (the pushing-away, the tone, the focus on his flaws rather than comfort) fits classic projection: people attack in others what they cannot accept in themselves. Her refusal to meet his need — despite knowing his despair — reads as self-protective (I will not be the outlet that confirms my own weakness). Film scholars note the cruelty is part of her defense mechanism. ”) (Asuka’s lines in the “hell kitchen” / pre-Instrumentality exchange hit directly at Shinji’s perceived laziness, cowardice, and neediness: “You don’t truly understand” / “You think you can save me?” / “Don’t come near me.” Those are accusatory lines that map onto Asuka’s own deepest self-doubts (fear of dependence, need for validation). The script/scene transcripts show this blunt, repeated confrontation. ”) Again argument is not that she should accept him absolutely.Its that Asuka's rejection is too harsh than it should,And being angry isn't a good reason. It's clearly that she is uncomfortable because Shinji is her shadow self.Its not like anger is not there.But the source of anger and the reason for such lashing out seems to be deffensive act. Rather than harsh rejection ,like she she first said things like “...... You are afraid of your mother.......”(whole dialogue) is a attack on his ego and his self image.And “Pathetic” and “No” is actually complete rejection of him but mostly it showed him how un needed and how he don't deserves to exist(the reason why called it attack is because there is difference between explaining and attacking).These 2 combined made him commit symbolic suicide(it's not that he is not in blame).No matter how pathetic one can be.They don't deserve a complete rejection in worse state of mind this way.What Shinji at that moment needed was *truth with compassion**That even if he is flawed he should exist*.As Asuka showed all

Article: Difference between INTRUMENTALITY and PRE INTRUMENTALITY

Article: Difference between INTRUMENTALITY and PRE INTRUMENTALITY
 try understand Shinji's pattern through the movie.Starting from hospital scene.We can see that Shinji is asking help from Asuka(Note that Shinji is in a aroused state,in front of Asuka,and aroused mean hyper activeness or maybe he is having anxiety).If he is asking help it means he indeed has will to live but in crumbled state. Or simply whenever Shinji is with Asuka he gets a hope of living.Now after hospital scene when Shinji thought that nobody will help him,then he actually got suicidal,as he lost hope,As the only hope i.e Asuka wasn't reacting.Hence it creates a illusion that he 'just wanna die' when ‘his suicidal thoughts varies with hope of getting help’.Now after when we get to Pre-Intrumentality.We see that now a part of hope again come sto Shinji.He is again hyper activeness around Asuka and is constantly asking for help.Meaning now he is again having hope of living.And he is actually waiting for a proper answer.Because now he will get a chance to live if Asuka accepts the offer. But when Asuka finally rejects him.With rejection she also made him feel like shit(worse thing you can do to a suicidal guy).He finally snaps and Suicides.As now he is sure that he will die. A very simple pattern.On basis of this it again becomes less likely wants to merge with Asuka at that moment.As he is not perfectly suicidal at that point of time but rather still has hope that he can be alive if she accepts him.And if he has hope,why would he wants to die by merging with her?When she accepts to live with him?To have intimacy with him? Even lyrics like orignal and Everything you've ever dreamed showed that Shinji wants to have intimacy rather than a merger.and it's secondary canon. “I wanted to kiss her ...” poetically either means to have intimacy or to express love. Now in intrumentality,As i said earlier that I can agree with you.But you also have to agree that both reason exists.That he wants to die and also merge.
